steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase “were more likely” is an auxiliary verb + comparative adjective that needs a specific verb form to follow.

steps.title: Apply the rule for likelihood adjectives
steps.content: An infinitive form is required after “more likely” because adjectives expressing likelihood take infinitive forms. The other choices—eaten (past participle), ate (simple past), and will be eating (future continuous)—violate this pattern. That matches choice C — C is correct.
